headlines.text: Trump calls ceasefire 'holding' as US and Iran trade fire in the Strait of Hormuz
UAE intercepts Iranian missiles and drones; Rubio awaits Tehran's response to peace proposal
US trade court strikes down Trump's 10% global tariffs for second time — government to refund $166B
US adds 115,000 jobs in April, crushing expectations of 65,000; markets rally
Gas prices hit $4.56/gallon national average — up 53% since Iran war began
Tennessee Republicans enact new House map carving up majority-Black Memphis district amid protests
Iran internet blackout enters Day 70 as NetBlocks reports 1,656+ hours of severed connectivity
The Devil Wears Prada 2 crosses $254M worldwide in its first week — Miranda Priestly remains undefeated
Chief Justice Roberts laments Supreme Court being seen as 'political actors' amid dimming public approval
NATO allies openly discussing a future without US leadership as Iran war fallout deepens

breaking.text: UAE air defenses engaged Iranian missiles and drones Friday; Rubio says US awaiting Iran's peace proposal response 'today'

stories.hed: Trump calls it a 'love tap' as US and Iran exchange fire across the Strait of Hormuz
stories.dek: Three US Navy destroyers were attacked by Iranian missiles and drones in the Strait of Hormuz, and the US responded with 'self-defense strikes' on Iranian military sites at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm. Trump told reporters the ceasefire was 'holding,' which is one definition of holding. Secretary of State Rubio flew to Rome to meet the Pope and await Tehran's peace proposal response, which has not yet arrived.

stories.hed: NATO allies quietly planning a post-US alliance as Iran war fractures the partnership
stories.dek: Fresh disputes between Washington and NATO over the Iran war are pushing European leaders to seriously consider a future without US leadership of the alliance. Canada's PM Mark Carney became the first non-European leader invited to a European Political Community summit, signaling a western realignment underway.

stories.hed: Trade court rules Trump's 10% global tariffs illegal — again — and orders $166B in refunds
stories.dek: A federal trade court ruled 2-1 that Trump's replacement 10% tariffs — imposed after the Supreme Court struck down his original tariffs in February — are also illegal and unauthorized by law. The government is now on the hook for over $166 billion in refunds to importers, with payments expected to begin next week.

stories.hed: Tennessee Republicans remap Memphis district as Democratic senator stands on her desk in protest
stories.dek: Amid raucous gallery protests, Tennessee Republicans enacted a new US House map that carves up a majority-Black Memphis district, benefiting the GOP ahead of November midterms. Democratic state Sen. Charlane Oliver stood on her desk holding a banner calling the redistricting a 'Jim Crow' effort before the chamber was quickly adjourned.

stories.hed: Trump is not getting what he wanted from the Iran war — and the goalposts have quietly moved
stories.dek: CNN analysis notes that Trump, who once demanded 'unconditional surrender' and regime change, is now pursuing a negotiated deal that falls well short of his original maximalist aims. His poll numbers have sunk to historic lows and gas is $4.56 a gallon, which is one way to lose a mandate.

markets.context: Markets rallied on a blowout April jobs report (115K vs. 65K expected) even as US-Iran clashes in the Strait of Hormuz kept oil above $100 Brent and nerves elevated.

gas.note: Gas is up 53% since February — your car now costs more to feed than your dog.

popCulture.hed: The Devil Wears Prada 2 struts past $254M worldwide in its first week, making Miranda Priestly the only person winning right now
popCulture.dek: The 20-years-later sequel reuniting Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci opened to $77M domestic and $234M globally — the second-biggest worldwide debut of 2026. Women made up 76% of ticket buyers, confirming that 'the cerulean sweater' speech holds more cultural power than most world leaders.

genZ.term: Italian Brainrot / Tung Tung Tung Sahur
genZ.definition: An AI-generated wooden creature holding a bat who chants an Indonesian Ramadan drumming call — part of the 'Italian Brainrot' meme universe of absurd AI characters with fake-Italian names like Bombardiro Crocodilo. The character went so viral it was added as a Fortnite skin in April 2026 and is now being applied to literally everything on TikTok.
genZ.usage: When Trump calls ongoing US-Iran missile exchanges a 'love tap' and insists the ceasefire is holding: 'Tung Tung Tung Sahur has entered the Strait of Hormuz.'

tldr: The US and Iran exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Day 70 of the war while Trump insisted the ceasefire was 'holding' and Rubio waited by the phone in Rome. Back home, a blowout jobs report lifted markets, courts killed Trump's tariffs again, and Americans paid $4.56 a gallon to drive to the theater to watch The Devil Wears Prada 2 — perhaps the most relatable response to current events.
